source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The one-dimensional quantum mechanical potential energy of a particle of mass $m$ is given by $$ V(x)=\begin{cases} V_{0}\delta(x), & -a<x<\infty \\ \infty, & x<-a \end{cases} $$ as shown in Fig. 1.19. At time $t=0$, the wave function of the particle is completely confined to the region $-a<x<0$. [Define the quantities $k=\sqrt{2 m E}/\hbar$ and $\alpha=2 m V_{0}/\hbar^{2}$] Give an expression for the time development of the wave function in terms of $f(k)$. What values of $k$ are expected to govern the time behavior at large times?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $\psi(x,t)=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(k)\psi_{k}(x)e^{-iE_{k}t/\hbar}dk,\quad \text{with small } k \text{ dominating at large } t$
extraction_notes: Question references Fig. 1.19, but the potential is also specified in the text and raw_record marks vision_relevance as optional.
No actual image file path or URL is provided; extracted the image field string representation.

image_dependency: 题干是量子力学势能与波函数演化的文字/公式描述，未显式要求读图或依赖空间视觉信息。
字段 `requires_image=false` 且 `text_dominant=true`，表明解题主要依赖文本而非图像。
虽然 `image_manifest` 中有 1 张图片，但更像题目截图/渲染载体，未见图表读数需求。
multi_step: 问题包含两个明确子任务：先写出用 `f(k)` 表示的时间演化，再判断大时间极限由哪些 `k` 主导。
需要结合边界势阱/δ 势背景与谱展开形式 `psi(x,t)=∫ f(k) psi_k(x)e^{-iE_k t/ħ} dk`，属于多步物理推理。
第二问关于 large-time behavior 还涉及对积分中主导波数区域的额外判断，不是单步代入。
verifiability: 答案形式较明确，包含一个标准积分展开表达式和关于大时间主导项的定性结论，可与参考答案逐项核对。
给定答案文本直接写出 `psi(x,t)=∫ f(k)psi_k(x)e^{-iE_k t/ħ}dk` 且说明 `small k` dominates，核验目标清晰。
`registry_passed=true`，题目与答案资源均存在，支持后续规则化检查。

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产均存在，`registry_passed=true` 且 `issues` 为空，核心语义支撑完整。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: The one-dimensional quantum mechanical potential energy of a particle of mass $m$ is given by $$ V(x)=\begin{cases} V_{0}\delta(x), & -a<x<\infty \\ \infty, & x<-a \end{cases} $$ as shown in Fig. 1.19. At time $t=0$, the wave function of the particle is completely confined to the region $-a<x<0$. [Define the quantities $k=\sqrt{2 m E}/\hbar$ and $\alpha=2 m V_{0}/\hbar^{2}$] Give an expression for the time development of the wave function in terms of $f(k)$. What values of $k$ are expected to govern the time behavior at large times?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $\psi(x,t)=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(k)\psi_{k}(x)e^{-iE_{k}t/\hbar}dk,\quad \text{with small } k \text{ dominating at large } t$
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: Question references Fig. 1.19, but the potential is also specified in the text and raw_record marks vision_relevance as optional.
No actual image file path or URL is provided; extracted the image field string representation.
metadata.reasoning_chain: (e) Since $$ \psi(x,0)=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(k)\psi_{k}(x)dk, $$ the time development is given by $$ \psi(x,t)=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(k)\psi_{k}(x)e^{-iE_{k}t/\hbar}dk, \quad E_{k}=\frac{\hbar^2k^2}{2m}. $$ For large times, the rapidly oscillating factor $e^{-iE_{k}t/\hbar}$ suppresses contributions from larger $k$, so that the small $k$ components dominate the behavior.
